successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exports LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, LeaveRequest, and CreateLeaveRequestDto exactly as specified in the phase architecture.
src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exports AuditAction, AuditRecord, and CreateAuditRecordDto exactly as specified in the phase architecture.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts defines AuditRepository and PostgreSqlAuditRepository using pg Pool and repository-pattern access.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts defines LeaveRepository and PostgreSqlLeaveRepository with createLeaveRequest, findById, and updateStatus methods.
createLeaveRequest(dto) persists a LeaveRequest with status 'PENDING' regardless of input DTO status and creates a corresponding AuditRecord with action 'CREATED'.
Vitest unit tests verify createLeaveRequest() stores a PENDING LeaveRequest, invokes audit record creation, and findById() returns the persisted LeaveRequest.
Project documentation is updated to describe LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, AuditAction, and the AuditRecord-to-LeaveRequest relationship.

constraints: Repository pattern: all database access must go through repository interfaces.
Audit records: all state-changing operations must write an audit record; createLeaveRequest() must create an AuditRecord with action 'CREATED'.
Use PostgreSQL-backed repository implementations via pg Pool.
Models and repositories must follow the exact interfaces and types defined in the provided phase architecture.
LeaveRequest status must be initialized as 'PENDING' when created.
Input validation must occur at API boundaries; do not introduce API-layer validation logic in repositories.
No sensitive data in logs; avoid logging PII or other sensitive information.
RBAC enforcement is a system-wide requirement but no API endpoints are introduced in this phase.
No unhandled promise rejections; async repository operations must handle errors appropriately.
Respect modular boundaries: establish leave and audit repository/model modules only; no reverse dependencies into leave from other modules.
outOfScope: Employee persistence changes or new employee models.
Leave workflow services such as submit, approve, or reject operations.
Balance deductions and approval lifecycle behavior.
Changes outside the specified leave and audit models, repositories, unit tests, and related documentation.
